<!-- Generated by the orchestrator-design Ultracode pass (10 agents). Architecture spec; implement against the phased plan in section 9. -->

# Showman Director / Orchestrator Architecture

This document is the single, implementable reconciliation of the five design drafts (topology, state-context, tool-catalog, assembly-contract, model-cost-failure) and the adversarial critique. Every contradiction the critique raised is resolved here to exactly one answer; where the original drafts disagreed, the rejected option is noted in the Decision Log (┬ž8) so nobody re-litigates it. Design bias throughout: the simplest thing that satisfies the hard constraints, reusing existing engine code and never modifying the engine.

---

## 1. Overview & the orchestrator Ôćö engine separation

The **engine** stays exactly as it is today: a deterministic, containerized render service that turns one `SceneSpec` into one MP4 (plus captions), content-addressed by `sha256(spec+options)`. We add a **separate orchestrator service/container** that is just another HTTP client of the engine. The orchestrator owns story, builder selection, spec assembly, fan-out, and final clip concatenation. It imports the builder libraries (`src/math`, `src/chem`, ÔÇŽ) as a *library* to emit nodes, but it ships finished `SceneSpec`s to the engine for pixels.

One new runtime fact the critique surfaced and we accept: **the orchestrator container needs FFmpeg on its PATH**, because final clip concatenation + narration mux happen orchestrator-side (see ┬ž5). The engine is untouched.


Key separation rules:
- The engine never sees orchestrator state (JobContext, continuity, plan). It only ever receives `SceneSpec` + `RenderOptions`.
- The orchestrator never recomputes the engine's cache key (it can't ÔÇö see ┬ž3). It submits specs and trusts the `cached` flag the engine already returns.
- All LLM calls reuse the `OpenRouterSpecAuthor` transport (default `openai/gpt-oss-120b`, swappable) behind a `SpecAuthor`-style proposer, wrapped in the existing `AuthoringAgent` proposeÔćĺvalidateÔćĺrepair loop.

---

## 2. Pipeline nodes & contracts

Four nodes. **Director** and **Scene Assembler** are deterministic (no LLM). **Lesson Planner** and **Domain Selector** are LLM nodes. Below are the final, canonical inter-node types ÔÇö these supersede every divergent shape in the drafts (critique #5, #6, #7).

### 2.0 Shared vocabulary (locked)

```ts
// One 5-value domain enum everywhere. "lesson" is NOT a domain ÔÇö whole-scene
// lesson builders are distinguished by the catalog `level: "scene"` axis (┬ž4).
export type Domain = "math" | "chem" | "physics" | "diagram" | "items";

export type Audience =
  | "elementary" | "middle" | "high" | "intro-college";
```

### 2.1 Director (deterministic control plane)

Responsibility: drive the state machine, enforce budgets, fan out per-scene work with bounded concurrency, handle retries/fallbacks, thread cross-scene continuity, call the engine over HTTP, trigger final concat+mux, persist `JobContext` after every node. No content decisions.

```ts
export interface Director {
  run(input: GenerateVideoInput): Promise<JobView>;   // returns the 202 projection
}
```

Internal flow (load-bearing):

```ts
async function direct(ctx: JobContext): Promise<void> {
  ctx.plan = await lessonPlanner.plan(ctx.request);     // 1 LLM call
  safetyGatePlan(ctx.plan);                             // fail-whole if unsafe (┬ž7)
  persist(ctx, "selecting");

  // Per-scene fan-out: independent, parallelizable, individually cache-keyed.
  await mapWithConcurrency(ctx.plan.scenes, SCENE_CONCURRENCY, async (beat) => {
    const placements = await domainSelector.select(beat, ctx.continuity, registry);
    const spec       = await sceneAssembler.assemble(placements, ctx, beat); // commits continuity
    const sceneState = ctx.scenes[beat.index];
    sceneState.spec  = spec;
  });
  persist(ctx, "rendering");

  await mapWithConcurrency(ctx.scenes, RENDER_CONCURRENCY, (s) =>
    renderScene(s, ctx));                               // silent clip + narration WAV, cached
  persist(ctx, "stitching");

  ctx.final = await assembleClips(ctx);                 // FFmpeg, orchestrator-side
  persist(ctx, "done");
  if (ctx.request.options?.webhook) fireWebhook(ctx);
}
```

### 2.2 Lesson Planner (LLM, 1 call/video)

```ts
export interface LessonPlanner {
  plan(req: ExternalRequest): Promise<LessonPlan>;
}

export interface LessonPlan {
  title: string;
  audience: Audience;
  theme: string;                 // chosen ONCE, propagated to every scene
  throughline: string;           // the single linking idea (recap anchor)
  goals: string[];               // learning objectives (for evals/self-check)
  scenes: SceneBeat[];           // ordered
  narrationArc: NarrationArc;    // cross-scene narration spine
  totalDurationBudgetSec: number;
  modelId: string;               // snapshot for reproducibility
}

// Canonical SceneBeat ÔÇö reconciles topology/state-context/model-cost (critique #6).
export interface SceneBeat {
  id: string;                    // "beat-1" ÔÇö stable across reorder
  index: number;                 // 0-based final order (load-bearing for offsets)
  title: string;
  goal: string;                  // pedagogical objective (NL) ÔÇö drives the Selector
  domainHint?: Domain;           // SOFT, single, optional
  keyPoints: string[];           // facts the visual must convey
  mustShow?: string[];           // optional HARD: entities/concepts required on screen
  mustReuse?: string[];          // optional HARD: recap keys from earlier beats
  forbid?: string[];             // optional HARD: builders/concepts to avoid
  narrationBeats: string[];      // talking points; Assembler turns into NarrationSegment[]
  durationBudgetSec: number;     // advisory; Assembler reconciles to real length
  dependsOn?: string[];          // beat ids, for recap/callback
}

export interface NarrationArc {
  intro?: string;
  outro?: string;
  transitions: Record<string /*beatId*/, string>;  // keyed by id, robust to reorder
}
```

`domainHint` is advisory (Selector may override, logged to history). `mustShow`/`mustReuse`/`forbid` are validated by the Director **after** selection; a violation is a retryable failure fed back into the Selector prompt via the existing correction-loop mechanism.

### 2.3 Domain Selector (LLM + typed catalog, 1 call/scene by default)

Replaces today's `parseMathBrief`/`parseBrief` regex dispatch with tool-selection over the typed catalog (┬ž4). Output is the **one canonical flat shape** (critique #5): `BuilderPlacement[]`.

```ts
export interface DomainSelector {
  select(beat: SceneBeat, continuity: ContinuityState,
         reg: BuilderRegistry): Promise<BuilderPlacement[]>;
}

// THE canonical selector output. Field names locked: builder, params, slot/at.
export interface BuilderPlacement {
  builder: string;                       // MUST be a catalog name, e.g. "chem.reaction"
  params: Record<string, unknown>;       // MUST validate against the tool's Zod schema
  slot?: "center" | "left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom" | "grid";  // declarative layout
  at?: { x: number; y: number };         // explicit override; assembler honors if present
  scale?: number;
  caption?: string;                      // on-canvas label
  ref?: string;                          // reuse a prior EntityRef by key (recall, ┬ž3)
}
```

Constraints enforced by the Selector (in order, cheapest first):
1. `builder` Ôłł catalog (constrained decoding via JSON-schema `enum` where the model supports it; else post-hoc validate + one re-select).
2. `params` type-check against the builder's Zod schema; `OUT_OF_RANGE` is **mechanically clamped, never sent back to the model** (mirrors `autoRepairSpec`).
3. A request is **either** one `level:"scene"` builder **or** N `level:"node"` builders ÔÇö never mixed (critique enforcement; the Assembler rejects mixed and re-prompts).

Selection mode rule (critique #13): if `beat.domainHint` is set Ôćĺ **one** LLM call with that domain's compact catalog. If the beat is cross-domain Ôćĺ two-stage (pick builders from the global compact digest, then fill params from the picked tools' full schemas). Default is one-call.

Offline / fallback selector (no API key, or LLM exhausted): the `KeywordSelector` scores `beat` text against `tool.keywords` and emits the same `BuilderPlacement[]`, routed through the **same** `registry.invoke` validation. Precedence: LLM selector Ôćĺ `KeywordSelector` Ôćĺ whole-scene `buildMathLesson` topic match Ôćĺ `buildCountingLesson`.

### 2.4 Scene Assembler (deterministic)

Turns `BuilderPlacement[]` into one validated `SceneSpec`. Pure, no LLM.

```ts
export interface SceneAssembler {
  assemble(placements: BuilderPlacement[], ctx: JobContext,
           beat: SceneBeat): Promise<SceneSpec>;
}
```

Algorithm:
1. **Invoke** each placement via `registry.invoke(builder, params)` (validates). If a `level:"scene"` builder was chosen (e.g. `buildQuadraticLesson` returns a full narrated `SceneSpec`), use it directly and skip steps 2ÔÇô4 ÔÇö just reconcile theme/narration/duration.
2. **Layout** node-level outputs by `slot`/`at` using each output's `bbox` (deterministic packing; ids already namespaced via `idGen`, so no collisions).
3. **Animate** via presets (`drawOn`, `fillStagger`, `countUp`, ÔÇŽ) Ôćĺ `Track[]`.
4. **Frame** the envelope: `specVersion`, `width/height/fps` from `continuity.canvas`, `background`/`theme`, `seed` = derived scene seed (┬ž3), `duration = max(animEnd, narrationLen, durationBudget)`.
5. **Narrate**: turn `beat.narrationBeats` + `narrationArc.transitions[beat.id]` + the previous scene's `RecapEntry.takeaway` (as `leadIn`) into `NarrationSegment[]` timed to the animation. This is the only cross-scene narration coupling, and it is one-directional (reads prior recap, never future) so scenes stay independently buildable.
6. **Validate + repair locally**: run the spec through the engine's validator (import or `POST /validate`), apply `autoRepairSpec` for mechanical fixes before shipping.
7. **Commit continuity**: only now (valid spec in hand) does the Assembler commit proposed `EntityRef`s and append a `RecapEntry` (critique-aligned: Selector proposes, Assembler commits).

Output: a render-ready `SceneSpec`.

### 2.5 Engine HTTP client

```ts
export interface EngineClient {
  validate(spec: SceneSpec): Promise<ValidationResult>;           // POST /validate
  preview(spec: SceneSpec, frame: number): Promise<PreviewResult>;// POST /preview (opt-in gate)
  render(spec: SceneSpec, opts: RenderOptions): Promise<RenderResult>; // POST /render (single-node, cached)
  submitDistributed(spec: SceneSpec, opts: RenderOptions): Promise<{jobId: string}>; // coordinator /jobs
  jobStatus(jobId: string): Promise<JobStatusView>;               // GET /jobs/:id
  objectUrl(key: string): string;                                 // GET /objects/:key
}

export interface RenderResult {
  video: StoredObject; durationSec: number; width: number; height: number; fps: number;
  cached: boolean;     // <-- THE cache signal the orchestrator uses (critique #3)
}
```

---

## 3. State & context model

### 3.1 JobContext (the durable source of truth)

One document per request, persisted to the existing `ObjectStorage` after every node, so the pipeline survives crash/retry. Reuses the `JobStore`/`Coordinator` durability pattern.

```ts
// One persisted job doc, projected to JobView for the API (critique #8).
export type JobPhase =
  | "queued" | "planning" | "selecting" | "assembling"
  | "rendering" | "stitching"        // single concat/mux phase (NOT 3 names)
  | "done" | "error";

export interface JobContext {
  // identity & idempotency
  jobId: string;                     // ULID; external handle
  request: ExternalRequest;          // { topic, query, options } ÔÇö frozen
  requestHash: string;               // sha256(canonical(request)) ÔÇö job dedup key
  rootSeed: number;                  // int(sha256(requestHash)) ÔÇö determinism root
  schemaVersion: 1;                  // context schema, NOT engine specVersion
  createdAt: string; updatedAt: string;

  // pipeline cursor (resume)
  phase: JobPhase;

  // node outputs
  plan?: LessonPlan;
  continuity: ContinuityState;       // trimmed (see ┬ž3.2)
  budget: TimeBudget;
  scenes: SceneState[];

  // final assembly
  final?: { videoKey: string; videoUrl: string; captionsKey?: string;
            captionsSrt?: string; durationSec: number; sceneOffsets: number[] };

  // diagnostics
  history: NodeRunRecord[];          // append-only audit of every node attempt
  warnings: string[];                // degradations, safety substitutions
  error?: { node: string; sceneIndex?: number; message: string; retryable: boolean };
}

export interface SceneState {
  index: number;
  beat: SceneBeat;
  placements?: BuilderPlacement[];   // Domain Selector output
  spec?: SceneSpec;                  // Assembler output
  localSpecHash?: string;            // orchestrator-OWN dedup hash, NOT the engine key
  narration: SceneNarration;
  render?: {
    status: "pending" | "running" | "done" | "error" | "cached" | "fallback";
    clipKey?: string;                // silent MP4 object key
    narrationKey?: string;           // cached WAV object key
    durationSec?: number;
    cached?: boolean;                // taken from RenderResult.cached
  };
  outcome: SceneOutcome;             // source/rung/degraded (┬ž7)
  attempts: number;
}
```

### 3.2 Cross-scene continuity (trimmed for v1 ÔÇö critique #14)

The heavy `entities/notation/regions/glossary` model is cut. For the concat architecture the only load-bearing continuity is shared visual identity + a one-directional recap thread.

```ts
export interface ContinuityState {
  theme: string;
  palette: { bg: string; fg: string; accent: string; muted: string };
  canvas: { width: number; height: number; fps: number };  // ONE canvas for all scenes
  recap: RecapEntry[];               // one per completed scene, ordered
}

export interface RecapEntry {
  sceneIndex: number;
  takeaway: string;                  // one sentence the next scene's leadIn may restate
}
```

`EntityRef`-style "re-render the exact same molecule in scene 4" is deferred. If/when a feature needs it, add a keyed `entities` map and store bulky params out-of-line at `contexts/{jobId}/entities/{key}.json`. The `BuilderPlacement.ref` field is reserved for that future; v1 selectors don't emit it.

Canvas is locked globally by the planner so `-c copy` concat is always available (┬ž5). Per-scene aspect changes are disallowed in v1.

### 3.3 Time budget

```ts
export interface TimeBudget {
  totalTargetSec: number;            // request or planner default (e.g. 90)
  hardMaxSec: number;                // ÔëĄ engine 600s cap minus margin
  reservedSec: number;               // intro/outro/transitions held back
  scenes: { index: number; targetSec: number; minSec: number; maxSec: number;
            actualSec?: number }[];
}
```

Planner allocates `targetSec` by weight; Assembler fills `actualSec`. If narration overruns `maxSec`, policy: **borrow from `reservedSec` first, trim narration only past `hardMaxSec`.** Director re-normalizes only the *remaining unbuilt* scenes so early scenes are never rebuilt due to late drift.

### 3.4 Async-job persistence & resume

```
contexts/{jobId}.json                  ÔćÉ full JobContext, rewritten after each node (single writer)
contexts/{jobId}/scene-{i}.spec.json   ÔćÉ built SceneSpec, immutable once written
```

Single writer per job (job-level lease, same mechanism as the coordinator's visibility-timeout queue for multi-replica orchestrators). Resume on pickup by inspecting `phase` + each `scenes[i].render.status`:
- `planning`/`selecting`/`assembling` unfinished Ôćĺ re-run that node; outputs overwrite, continuity commits are key-merged (idempotent).
- `rendering` Ôćĺ **do not resubmit if a clip already exists.** Resubmitting an unchanged spec to the single-node `/render` is a guaranteed engine cache hit anyway (the engine keys on `sha256(spec+options)`), so retries are near-free.
- `stitching` Ôćĺ re-run; concat is deterministic over the same ordered clip keys.

Idempotency keys: job dedup `requestHash`; scene dedup orchestrator-local `localSpecHash` (for skipping re-selection/re-assembly only); engine clip reuse is the engine's own content-addressing.

### 3.5 Determinism approach

The engine is byte-deterministic per spec. The orchestrator's only nondeterminism is the LLM nodes. We make runs reproducible-enough by:

1. **Seed-derivation tree.** `rootSeed = int(sha256(requestHash))`; `sceneSeed = sha256(rootSeed + ":" + index)` written into `SceneSpec.seed`. Same request Ôćĺ same seeds Ôćĺ identical specs *if* LLM output is identical.
2. **Immutable per-scene specs = the real determinism boundary.** Once `contexts/{jobId}/scene-{i}.spec.json` exists it is never re-derived within that job. So a job is fully reproducible after its first pass; the LLM is nondeterministic only on the very first build of each scene.
3. **Cross-request reproducibility (best-effort, v1).** Set LLM `temperature` low (0.2 selector, 0.4 planner) and snapshot `modelId` + prompt-dir hash in the context. An optional LLM-response cache (`llm-cache/{sha256(model+promptHash+inputHash)}.json` + a manual `cacheEpoch` bust integer) is **deferred** ÔÇö temperature + pinning is enough for v1.

Critically (critique #3): **the orchestrator does NOT recompute the engine `jobKey`.** That key is computed engine-side *after* `inlineAssets`, optional `fitNarration` duration mutation, `interactions` stripping, and folds in `hasTts: !!this.tts` ÔÇö engine-internal state the orchestrator can't see. The orchestrator's `localSpecHash` is for its own `contexts/` dedup only. Whether a render was a cache hit comes from `RenderResult.cached`, full stop.

---

## 4. Builder tool-catalog

The key refactor: replace regex dispatch with a typed registry of self-describing, self-validating builder wrappers. New module **`src/orchestrator/catalog/`** (keeps the Zod dependency out of the engine container ÔÇö critique #11).

### 4.1 BuilderTool interface (Zod-sourced)

Zod is the single source of truth: runtime validation (`.safeParse`), JSON-Schema emission (`zod-to-json-schema` for the LLM), and cross-field refinements (`from < to`, `pH Ôłł [0,14]`, `order Ôłł {1,2,3}`) all derive from one schema. A flat `BuilderParam[]` descriptor is derived from it *only* for display/eval, never authored by hand (critique #11).

```ts
// src/orchestrator/catalog/types.ts
import type { ZodType } from "zod";
import type { Node } from "../../spec/types";

export interface BuilderOutput {
  node: Node;                              // handles (NumberLine/Plane/Box) reduced to .node
  bbox?: { w: number; h: number };         // local extents for auto-layout
  handle?: unknown;                        // concrete handle for macro-internal composition only
}

export interface BuilderTool<P = unknown> {
  name: string;                            // "math.numberLine", "chem.reaction"
  domain: Domain;
  level: "scene" | "node";                 // scene = returns full SceneSpec; node = placeable
  description: string;                     // one terse line: what it DEPICTS (feeds the LLM)
  keywords: string[];                      // offline-selector matching (replaces regex intents)
  params: ZodType<P>;                      // THE source of truth
  build(params: P): BuilderOutput;         // node-level: wraps the pure builder
  buildScene?(params: P): SceneSpec;       // scene-level only (e.g. buildQuadraticLesson)
  example?: P;
}
```

### 4.2 Registry

```ts
// src/orchestrator/catalog/registry.ts
export class BuilderRegistry {
  private tools = new Map<string, BuilderTool>();
  register<P>(t: BuilderTool<P>): void { /* throw on dup name */ }
  get(name: string): BuilderTool | undefined { return this.tools.get(name); }
  list(domain?: Domain): BuilderTool[] { /* filtered */ }

  invoke(name: string, raw: unknown): BuilderOutput {        // node-level
    const t = this.tools.get(name);
    if (!t) throw new CatalogError("UNKNOWN_BUILDER", name);
    const p = t.params.safeParse(raw);
    if (!p.success) throw new CatalogError("INVALID_PARAMS", name, p.error.issues);
    return t.build(p.data as never);
  }

  jsonSchema(name: string): object | undefined { /* zodToJsonSchema, sorted keys */ }
}
export const registry = new BuilderRegistry();
```

Registration is **explicit** (each domain ships `register.ts`; a barrel imports them for side effects) ÔÇö greppable, deterministic order (matters for stable catalog hashing), test-friendly.

### 4.3 Token-frugal digest (mirrors `describeSceneCompact`)

`describeCatalogCompact(reg, domains?)` emits ~1 line/builder (`name | depicts | key params`), filtered to the beat's domain so the prompt carries ~10ÔÇô25 tools, not all ~121. Full per-tool JSON-Schema is fetched lazily only for picked builders (Stage B / param-fill). Catalog digest is content-hashed (with sorted keys to avoid `zod-to-json-schema` ordering nondeterminism) and prompt-cached.

### 4.4 Honest scope (critique #10)

The catalog is **not** a mechanical 1:1 of 121 builders. Flat builders (`reaction` taking mhchem `string[]`, `numberLine` taking `from/to/step/width`) wrap cleanly. But composite/handle builders (`coordinatePlane` + `plotFunction` plotted via `toX`) need **macro builders** (`math.functionGraph` wraps plane+plot+axes as one catalog entry; the primitive pair stays internal, using `handle`). This is hand-curation. Whole-scene lesson builders (`buildMathLesson`'s 13 topics) enter as `level:"scene"` tools. **Start with the high-value subset** ÔÇö the 14 lesson topics + ~15 chem/physics scene/visual builders ÔÇö and grow lazily. CI check: every `BuilderTool` round-trips its own `example` through `invoke`.

### 4.5 Worked migration: `math.numberLine`

Underlying builder unchanged: `numberLine(opts): { node, toX, ... }`.

```ts
// src/orchestrator/catalog/math/numberLine.tool.ts
import { z } from "zod";
import { numberLine } from "../../../math/builders";
import type { BuilderTool } from "../types";

const Params = z.object({
  from: z.number().describe("left end value"),
  to:   z.number().describe("right end value"),
  step: z.number().positive().default(1).describe("tick spacing"),
  width: z.number().positive().max(7680).default(400),
  theme: z.string().optional(),
}).refine((p) => p.to !== p.from, { message: "from and to must differ", path: ["to"] });

export const numberLineTool: BuilderTool<z.infer<typeof Params>> = {
  name: "math.numberLine", domain: "math", level: "node",
  description: "horizontal number line with evenly spaced ticks and labels",
  keywords: ["number line", "integer", "count on a line", "interval"],
  params: Params,
  example: { from: 0, to: 10, step: 1, width: 400 },
  build(p) {
    const nl = numberLine(p);                       // existing pure builder
    return { node: nl.node, bbox: { w: p.width, h: 48 }, handle: nl.toX };
  },
};
```

What changes elsewhere: the regex case in `src/authoring/mathBrief.ts` that matched "ÔÇŽnumber lineÔÇŽ" and hand-extracted `from/to/step` is **deleted from the LLM path**; its intent keywords move verbatim into `keywords` so the offline `KeywordSelector` still matches. Both LLM and offline paths funnel through `registry.invoke` Ôćĺ byte-identical output.

---

## 5. Multi-scene assembly

**Decision (critique #1, #2): clip-level concat, orchestrator-side, silent clips + per-scene WAV + single final mux.** The "reuse the distributed concat assembler" constraint rests on a mistaken premise ÔÇö `assembleSegments` streams *decompressed raw RGBA of one spec* through one encode with uniform dims and **no audio**; it physically cannot stitch N finished, differently-specced MP4 clips. We therefore reuse its *FFmpeg discipline* (`buildEncodeArgs`, SIGKILL-on-error) and the existing `muxAudioVideo`, not the function itself. This is the explicit reframing flagged to the user: **"reuse concat assembler" Ôćĺ "reuse `buildEncodeArgs` + `muxAudioVideo`," and the orchestrator container gains FFmpeg.**

### 5.1 Per-scene render: silent clips

Each scene renders **silent** (`narrate: false`), so the clip cache key is voice-independent and re-usable, and `-c copy` concat has no audio streams to align. Narration is synthesized to a **separate cached WAV**.

```ts
// keys
clipKey      = engine-owned, content-addressed by sha256(spec, {ÔÇŽ, narrate:false})  // existing
narrationKey = audio/{sha256({ segments, voice })}.wav                               // new
```

```ts
export interface RenderedScene {
  index: number;
  clip: StoredObject;          // silent MP4 (cached engine-side)
  narrationWav?: StoredObject; // cached separately by narrationKey
  durationSec: number;         // authoritative: frameCount/fps
  width: number; height: number; fps: number;
  status: "ok" | "fallback";
}
```

**Long-scene routing (critique #9):** route to the single-node `/render` path by default to preserve content-addressed caching. The distributed coordinator keys segments by `jobId`, not `sha256(spec+opts)`, so it has **no cross-run clip cache** ÔÇö use it only as overflow for genuinely huge scenes, and record those scenes as explicitly uncached. Don't claim cache reuse for distributed scenes.

### 5.2 Concatenation + narration mux

`assembleClips` (new `src/orchestrator/assembleClips.ts`) has two modes:

- **Default ÔÇö all cuts, uniform codec params Ôćĺ FFmpeg concat demuxer with stream copy** (`-f concat -safe 0 -i list.txt -c copy`). No re-encode, fast, byte-deterministic. Always available because canvas/fps/crf/preset are locked globally per job (┬ž3.2).
- **Fade or mixed dims Ôćĺ filter graph** (`xfade` video + `acrossfade`/delay+amix audio) Ôćĺ single re-encode via the same `buildEncodeArgs`.

Narration: place each scene's WAV at its cumulative offset into one combined WAV, then a single `muxAudioVideo(silentConcat, combinedWav)`. Captions globalized by adding `sceneOffsets[i]` to each scene's `segmentDurations` (from `synthesizeNarration`), emitted via existing `toVTT`/`toSRT`.

**Determinism (per the user's golden-determinism memory):** stream-copy concat is byte-deterministic ÔÇö keep it the default. The xfade re-encode path (and large cross-clip gradient fades especially) is **not** guaranteed byte-identical cross-platform; mark fade output **non-cached, best-effort cosmetic**. The final concatenated video is not cached by default (cheap relative to per-scene renders); the per-scene clips carry the cache value.

### 5.3 Per-scene failure isolation

A fallback scene **keeps its slot** (same `index`, sane `durationSec`) so offsets and narration timing never shift. Levels:
1. **Author/select failure** (no valid placement after max attempts) Ôćĺ deterministic template card (`TextNode` title + beat, or a `buildMathLesson` primitive). Always validates. `status:"fallback"`.
2. **Render failure** on a valid spec Ôćĺ retry once Ôćĺ else placeholder clip of identical dims/duration.
3. **Scene-level safety rejection (422)** Ôćĺ substitute a neutral card + log to `warnings` (degrade-to-card, NOT fail-whole ÔÇö see ┬ž7).

A job fails wholesale only if the planner yields zero scenes or **all** scenes fall back.

```ts
export interface SceneOutcome {
  index: number;
  source: "builder" | "free-author" | "fallback";
  status: "ok" | "repaired" | "fallback" | "dropped";
  rung: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8;        // where it settled in the ladder (┬ž7)
  attempts: number; llmCalls: number;
  repairs: string[]; warnings: string[];
  degraded: boolean;            // true at rung 8
  previewPassed?: boolean;
}
```

---

## 6. External async tool contract

Extend `showman_generate_video` / `POST /v1/generate` rather than inventing a surface. Multi-scene renders are long Ôćĺ async by default. The `{ brief }` single-scene form keeps working for back-compat (still returns a `jobId` for a uniform surface).

### 6.1 Submit

```jsonc
// POST /v1/generate  (and MCP showman_generate_video)
{
  "topic": "photosynthesis",
  "query": "explain the light-dependent reactions for a 9th grader",
  "options": {
    "audience": "high",
    "maxScenes": 6,                 // hard cap 8
    "targetDurationSec": 90,
    "theme": "meadow",
    "voice": "narrator-en-1",
    "transition": "cut",            // default cut; "fade" opts into xfade (non-deterministic)
    "deterministic": true,
    "crf": 18, "preset": "medium",
    "locale": "en",
    "previewGate": false,           // opt-in; only ever runs on free-author scenes
    "webhook": "https://caller/hook"
  }
}
```

```jsonc
// 202
{ "jobId": "gen_01J...", "status": "queued",
  "statusUrl": "/v1/jobs/gen_01J...", "kind": "multi-scene" }
```

```ts
export interface GenerateVideoInput { topic: string; query: string; options?: GenerateVideoOptions; }
export interface ExternalRequest extends GenerateVideoInput {}  // frozen in JobContext
```

### 6.2 Poll ÔÇö `GET /v1/jobs/:id` (JobView projection of JobContext)

```jsonc
{
  "jobId": "gen_01J...",
  "status": "rendering",
  "phase": { "name": "rendering", "scenesDone": 3, "scenesTotal": 6 },
  "scenes": [
    { "index": 0, "status": "ok",       "durationSec": 7.2 },
    { "index": 1, "status": "repaired", "durationSec": 9.0 },
    { "index": 2, "status": "rendering" },
    { "index": 3, "status": "fallback", "warnings": ["safety: substituted card"] },
    { "index": 4, "status": "queued" },
    { "index": 5, "status": "queued" }
  ],
  "progress": { "framesDone": 540, "totalFrames": 1280 },
  "warnings": [],
  "result": null,
  "createdAt": "...", "updatedAt": "..."
}
```

On `done`:

```jsonc
{
  "status": "done",
  "result": {
    "video":       { "key": "videos/gen_...mp4", "url": "/objects/videos/gen_...mp4" },
    "captions":    { "key": "videos/gen_...vtt", "url": "/objects/..." },
    "captionsSrt": { "key": "videos/gen_...srt", "url": "/objects/..." },
    "durationSec": 49.4, "width": 1920, "height": 1080, "fps": 30,
    "sceneOffsets": [0, 7.2, 16.2, 25.2, 32.4, 41.0],
    "outline": [ { "index": 0, "title": "...", "goal": "..." } ],
    "warnings": [ "scene 3 used fallback card" ]
  }
}
```

### 6.3 Job state machine (one canonical phase enum ÔÇö critique #8)

```
queued Ôćĺ planning Ôćĺ selecting Ôćĺ assembling Ôćĺ rendering Ôćĺ stitching Ôćĺ done
                                                  Ôöé
            (planner empty | all scenes dropped)  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ error
```

- Phase transitions are monotonic. `concatenating`/`muxing`/`stitching` collapse to the single name **`stitching`**.
- `rendering` is the only phase with per-scene sub-progress; `scenesDone` increments as each `RenderedScene` resolves (ok or fallback).
- A single scene's render error resolves to `fallback`, **not** job `error`.

### 6.4 Webhook (one place, hardened)

If `options.webhook` is set, POST the terminal `JobView` once with header `X-Showman-Job` and an HMAC signature (`SHOWMAN_WEBHOOK_SECRET`). Polling remains the source of truth. Noted surfaces to handle in implementation: **SSRF** (caller-supplied URL ÔÇö apply an allowlist / block internal ranges), and **delivery state persisted in JobContext** (`webhookDeliveredAt`) so a crash mid-delivery doesn't double-fire or silently drop. At-least-once beyond a single attempt is deferred; callers needing guarantees poll.

---

## 7. Model / cost / failure ladder + evals

### 7.1 Per-node model tiers

Default everywhere: `openai/gpt-oss-120b` via `OpenRouterSpecAuthor` (swappable). Escalation is a config knob, off by default.

| Node | Model | Temp | Output budget | Escalation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lesson Planner | gpt-oss-120b | 0.4 | ~1200 tok | strong model only if `query` long/ambiguous or `scenes>8` (config, off) |
| Domain Selector | gpt-oss-120b | 0.2 | ~500 tok | none (bounded by catalog + ladder) |
| Free-author (fallback only) | gpt-oss-120b | ÔÇö | ~2500 tok | strong model after 2 failed attempts (config) |

Cost levers: catalog pre-filtering (Selector sees ~15 tools not 121, ~10├Ś prompt shrink on the hot node); prompt-cache the static planner/selector system blocks + compact schema; engine render-cache reuse (pure builders Ôćĺ identical specs Ôćĺ free clips ÔÇö never inject timestamps/random ids into specs); `maxLlmCallsPerScene`/`maxLlmCallsPerVideo` ceilings that force rung 8 instead of looping; bounded scene parallelism.

### 7.2 Per-scene quality / failure ladder

Cheapest-and-most-deterministic first; the LLM is the last resort before the deterministic net. **Most scenes never hit a free-form authoring model** ÔÇö the catalog's whole purpose.

```
SceneBeat
  Ôľ╝ (1) Selector Ôćĺ BuilderPlacement[]            [LLM, cheap, constrained to catalog]
  Ôľ╝ (2) param validation (Zod)                   [pure] Ôćĺ clamp OUT_OF_RANGE; unknown builder Ôćĺ 1 re-select
  Ôľ╝ (3) build()                                  [pure builder, no LLM]
  Ôľ╝ (4) validate + autoRepairSpec                [pure, reused]
  Ôľ╝ (5) preview-gate (blank detection)           [engine /preview] ÔÇö ONLY for free-author scenes (critique #12)
  Ôľ╝ (6) accept
        Ôöö on exhaustion of (1)-(5):
  Ôľ╝ (7) bounded LLM re-correct                    [feed errors back to Selector or drop to free-author]
  Ôľ╝ (8) deterministic fallback                    [buildMathLesson / buildCountingLesson ÔÇö always valid]
```

Rung notes:
- **(5) preview-gate runs only on free-author scenes** (critique #12). A deterministic builder-path scene cannot be "blank" unless params are degenerate (already caught at rung 2), so paying an `/preview` round-trip ├Ś every scene is waste. Builder-path scenes skip it. The gate is also globally opt-in via `options.previewGate` for quality-sensitive runs.
- **(8) guarantees every scene yields a renderable spec.** A degraded scene beats a failed video.

### 7.3 Content-safety (layered ÔÇö resolves the ┬ž6-vs-┬ž4 contradiction, critique #4)

One policy, by layer:
- **Plan layer = fail-whole, fail-fast.** Moderate `{topic, query}` and the full `LessonPlan` (all `goal`/`narrationBeats` text) **before** any scene fan-out. Unsafe topic Ôćĺ whole `/v1/generate` job fails with `content_safety` + offending beats. Cheapest place to stop.
- **Scene layer = degrade-to-card.** An LLM-authored scene that trips the engine's existing per-spec gate (422) is replaced by a neutral card + `warnings` entry ÔÇö it does **not** nuke an otherwise-good multi-scene lesson.
- **Narration/TTS layer.** Re-check final spoken text per scene before mux.

A child-audience profile (from `plan.audience`) tightens rules. `allowPartial` is implicit: plan-layer is always fail-closed; scene-layer is always degrade. No contradictory defaults.

### 7.4 Evals hooks

Extends the existing `docs/planning/EVALS_AND_CI.md` (same Vitest two-tier offline/live shape, scorecard artifacts, nightly+manual cadence). New brief suite `test/evals/orchestrator.briefs.ts` of `{topic, query}` pairs incl. adversarial/underspecified.

- **Planner:** plan validity (hard 100%), scene-count sanity (hard), theme consistency (hard), beat coherence (LLM-judge, live tier, soft).
- **Selector (highest-value, gradeable offline):** builder-choice correctness vs a frozen **acceptable-set** label per beat (not single answer ÔÇö "3/4" Ôćĺ pie *or* bar *or* number-line all valid), top-1/top-set thresholds; builder-id validity (hard 100%); param clamp-rate tracked like mechanical-repair rate; built-spec validity (hard).
- **End-to-end:** per-scene render success; **degraded-scene rate** (`outcome.degraded`, soft ceiling); concat success + duration; tokens/latency budget.
- **The single best health metric:** per-video scorecard of `source` distribution ÔÇö "85% builder-path / 10% free-author / 5% fallback." Regression-track it; it directly measures whether the catalog refactor is working.

Offline orchestrator evals run in normal CI with `ScriptedAuthor` + a scripted plan (no key); live LLM-judged evals are manual+nightly behind `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

---

## 8. Decision log

| # | Decision | Chosen option | One-line rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Concat path** | New orchestrator `assembleClips.ts` reusing `buildEncodeArgs` + `muxAudioVideo`; FFmpeg in the orchestrator container. No engine change. | `assembleSegments` is raw-RGBA/single-spec/no-audio and *cannot* stitch finished clips; a new engine `/concat` would violate "don't change the engine." |
| 2 | **Narration model** | Silent clips (`narrate:false`) + per-scene cached WAV + single final mux. | Makes `-c copy` concat trivial, keeps clip cache voice-independent, and is the only model compatible with the chosen concat path. |
| 3 | **Engine cache** | Orchestrator never recomputes `jobKey`; reads `RenderResult.cached`; uses a local hash only for its own `contexts/` dedup. | The engine key folds in post-inline/post-fitNarration/`hasTts` state the orchestrator can't see ÔÇö recomputation silently diverges. |
| 4 | **Safety policy** | Fail-whole at plan layer; degrade-to-card at scene/narration layer. | Kill unsafe topics cheaply pre-fanout; don't let one bad authored scene nuke a good multi-scene lesson. |
| 5 | **Selector output type** | One canonical flat `BuilderPlacement[]` (`builder`/`params`/`slot`/`at`). | A flat array is as expressive as primary+`compose`; the Assembler already consumes it; ends 4-way field-name drift. |
| 6 | **Plan/Beat shape** | One `LessonPlan`/`SceneBeat` with both `id`+`index`, single optional `domainHint`, `narrationBeats[]`, id-keyed transitions, optional `mustShow/mustReuse/forbid`. | `index` is load-bearing for concat offsets; id-keyed spine survives reorder; one struct kills three divergent definitions. |
| 7 | **Domain enum** | 5 values; drop `"lesson"`. | Whole-scene builders are modeled by the catalog `level:"scene"` axis, not a fake domain. |
| 8 | **Job model + phases** | One persisted `JobContext` Ôćĺ `JobView`; one monotonic phase enum with single `stitching`. | Three job docs and three phase vocabularies collapse to one source of truth. |
| 9 | **Catalog type system + location** | Zod-sourced, derive display descriptors; live at `src/orchestrator/catalog/`. | Single source of truth for validate + JSON-Schema + cross-field refinements; keeps Zod out of the engine container. |
| 10 | **Catalog scope** | Curated subset + hand-authored macro builders; not mechanical 1:1├Ś121. | Composite/handle builders need macros; start high-value (14 lesson topics + ~15 sci builders), grow lazily. |
| 11 | **Preview-gate** | Free-author scenes only (plus global opt-in). | Deterministic builder scenes can't go blank; per-scene `/preview` ├Ś N is wasted latency. |
| 12 | **Selector call mode** | One call when `domainHint` set; two-stage only cross-domain. | Default single call is cheapest; two-stage reserved for the genuinely ambiguous case. |
| 13 | **Continuity scope (v1)** | Trim to `{theme, palette, canvas, recap}`. | Entities/notation/regions fight the independent-scene/concat model and aren't load-bearing yet. |
| 14 | **Long-scene routing** | Single-node `/render` default (cacheable); coordinator as overflow, explicitly uncached. | The distributed path keys by `jobId`, has no cross-run clip cache ÔÇö don't claim reuse there. |
| 15 | **Transitions / determinism** | Default `cut` (stream-copy, byte-deterministic, cached); `fade` is non-cached best-effort. | Honors the golden-determinism rule that large cross-clip gradient fades aren't byte-safe cross-platform. |

---

## 9. Phased refactor plan

Each phase is one PR-sized, independently shippable, CI-green increment. Earlier phases land value without the LLM. "Reused / Added / Changed" is called out per phase. The engine is **never changed** in any phase.

### Phase 0 ÔÇö Scaffolding & types (no behavior change)
- **Add:** `src/orchestrator/` package skeleton; `types.ts` with the locked shared types (`Domain`, `LessonPlan`, `SceneBeat`, `BuilderPlacement`, `JobContext`, `JobPhase`, `SceneOutcome`, etc.); add `zod` + `zod-to-json-schema` deps to the orchestrator package only.
- **Reused:** existing `SceneSpec`/`RenderOptions` types imported from `src/spec`.
- **Changed:** none.
- **Ship test:** type-compile + a trivial JobContext round-trip to `contexts/{jobId}.json`.

### Phase 1 ÔÇö Builder catalog core + first wrappers
- **Add:** `catalog/{types,registry,describe}.ts`; `math/numberLine.tool.ts`, `chem/reaction.tool.ts`, and the 14 `level:"scene"` `buildMathLesson` topic wrappers; per-domain `register.ts`; CI check that every tool's `example` round-trips `invoke`.
- **Reused:** all underlying pure builders (`numberLine`, `reaction`, `buildMathLesson`) unchanged.
- **Changed:** none yet (regex path still live).
- **Ship test:** registry unit tests; `describeCatalogCompact` snapshot; offline invoke of every registered tool.

### Phase 2 ÔÇö Deterministic Scene Assembler + layout
- **Add:** `assembler/place.ts` (`placeBuilders`, `layoutSlot`), `SceneAssembler.assemble` for node-level and scene-level placements; local validate + `autoRepairSpec` call.
- **Reused:** `autoRepairSpec`, engine `validate` (HTTP or import), `idGen`, presets for animation.
- **Changed:** none.
- **Ship test:** golden `SceneSpec` from a fixed `BuilderPlacement[]`; mixed scene/node request rejected.

### Phase 3 ÔÇö Offline KeywordSelector + end-to-end single-scene (no LLM)
- **Add:** `select/selector.ts` `DomainSelector` interface + `KeywordSelector` (scores `tool.keywords`, emits `BuilderPlacement[]`, validates via `registry.invoke`).
- **Reused:** the coefficient-extraction regexes from `mathBrief.ts` (moved behind the keyword selector), `buildMathLesson`/`buildCountingLesson` as fallback tiers.
- **Changed:** `src/authoring/mathBrief.ts` ÔÇö delete the regex *dispatch* branches that are now catalog tools; keep coefficient parsers as `extractParams` helpers.
- **Ship test:** parity test ÔÇö for a corpus of briefs, KeywordSelectorÔćĺAssembler produces specs equivalent to today's `parseMathBrief`Ôćĺ`buildMathLesson`.

### Phase 4 ÔÇö Clip assembly (silent clips + WAV + mux), single scene
- **Add:** `assembleClips.ts` (concat demuxer cut path first; xfade deferred), `narrationCache.ts`; orchestrator FFmpeg dependency + container update.
- **Reused:** `buildEncodeArgs` discipline, `muxAudioVideo`, `synthesizeNarration`, `toVTT`/`toSRT`.
- **Changed:** none in engine.
- **Ship test:** concat two fixed silent clips + two WAVs Ôćĺ byte-deterministic MP4 with globalized captions; verify `ftyp`/duration/`sceneOffsets`.

### Phase 5 ÔÇö Director state machine + async job surface (offline planner)
- **Add:** `pipeline.ts` Director driver (planningÔćĺÔÇŽÔćĺstitching), `JobContext` persistence + resume, `POST /v1/generate` accepting `{topic,query}`, `GET /v1/jobs/:id`, a **stub/offline planner** (deterministic single-beat or template plan) so the whole pipe runs with zero API key.
- **Reused:** `JobStore`/coordinator durability pattern, content-safety gate (plan + scene layers), `AuthoringAgent` loop for the free-author fallback rung.
- **Changed:** `src/mcp/showmanTools.ts` ÔÇö extend `showman_generate_video` input to `{topic,query,options}` + `jobId`/`statusUrl` output; keep `{brief}` back-compat.
- **Ship test:** offline end-to-end `{topic,query}` Ôćĺ multi-scene video via template plan; crash-resume from each phase.

### Phase 6 ÔÇö LLM Lesson Planner + Domain Selector
- **Add:** `plan.ts`, LLM `DomainSelector` (constrained decoding, one-call/two-stage rule), prompts `planner-system.md`/`planner-correction.md`/`selector-system.md`/`selector-correction.md`.
- **Reused:** `OpenRouterSpecAuthor` transport, `loadPrompts`+`SHOWMAN_PROMPT_DIR`, `jsonRepair`, the proposeÔćĺvalidateÔćĺrepair loop, per-scene quality ladder rungs 1ÔÇô8.
- **Changed:** Director swaps the stub planner for the LLM planner (selection precedence keeps offline fallback).
- **Ship test:** live-tier eval behind `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`; offline CI uses `ScriptedAuthor` + scripted plan.

### Phase 7 ÔÇö Hardening & polish
- **Add:** webhook delivery (HMAC + SSRF allowlist + `webhookDeliveredAt` persistence), xfade fade transition path (non-cached), preview-gate for free-author scenes, full evals scorecard incl. `source`-distribution metric, budget reconciliation policy.
- **Reused:** everything above.
- **Changed:** incremental config knobs (`SHOWMAN_SCENE_CONCURRENCY`, escalation models, `maxLlmCallsPer*`).
- **Ship test:** webhook fired once on terminal state; degraded-scene rate + source distribution in nightly scorecard.

**Reuse summary across the plan:** unchanged reuse of `RenderService.render`/`jobKey` (called, never recomputed), `autoRepairSpec`, `AuthoringAgent`, `synthesizeNarration`, `muxAudioVideo`, `buildEncodeArgs`, `toVTT`/`toSRT`, content-safety gate, `loadPrompts`, `jsonRepair`, `describeSceneCompact` (as the pattern for `describeCatalogCompact`), the JobStore/coordinator durability pattern, and the offline lesson builders as the deterministic fallback net. The only genuinely new asset is the **typed builder catalog** plus the thin orchestrator service around it ÔÇö exactly the regex-dispatchÔćĺtool-catalog refactor the project set out to do.
